events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario. Field service, timing & mechanical, timing marks misaligned by a tooth. Work rough-complete. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous. Risk high/critical? Timing off by a tooth can cause poor performance, engine damage, valve-to-piston interference. Critical maybe. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80. Value: misalignment by one tooth. Confidence high. Findings: timing marks off by one tooth relative to OEM spec. Actions: re-time cam/crank per OEM, verify alignment, rotate engine by hand, reassemble. Rationale. References: OEM service info. Need include all fields. sound_source etc not relevant but schema requires values; use defaults empty string, 0, empty list. Need valid JSON only. Ensure no prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 1,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["Timing marks are misaligned by one tooth relative to OEM timing reference", "Incorrect valve timing can cause rough running, loss of power, and potential valve-to-piston contact"],
  "actions": ["Reposition timing belt/chain and align all timing marks per OEM service information", "Rotate engine by hand through two full revolutions and recheck mark alignment", "Clear any related fault codes and verify engine operation before close-out"],
  "rationale": "A one-tooth timing error exceeds the zero-tolerance alignment requirement specified by the OEM; even a single tooth changes cam/crank phasing enough to create incorrect valve timing, risking engine damage and non-compliance with service specifications.",
  "references": ["OEM service information - timing mark alignment procedure"]
}
